name: New language proposal
about: Propose adding a TZUR Original display wordlist for a new language
title: "[new-language] <language>"
labels: new-language

### Language

<!-- Name of the language and BCP-47 code (e.g., "Yoruba, yo"). -->

### Script

<!-- e.g., Latin, Cyrillic, Arabic, Devanagari, Hebrew, Han. Note any diacritics or combining marks. -->

### Prior BIP-39 wordlist

<!-- Is there an existing BIP-39 wordlist in this language (canonical spec, community, or third-party)? Link if so. -->

### Native-speaker reviewer

<!-- Availability of a fluent native speaker to review the wordlist before merge. Required for acceptance. -->

### Motivation

<!-- Why this language matters for BIP-39 display-layer coverage. Audience size, ecosystem presence, existing wallet demand. -->

### Scope confirmation

- [ ] I understand all TZUR Original wordlists are index-paired semantic translations of the canonical English BIP-39 wordlist.
- [ ] I understand the seed is derived from the canonical English mnemonic, not the display mnemonic.
- [ ] I am prepared to submit the wordlist, mapping, test vector, and native-speaker attestation together in a single PR.
